title: Radiology AI Platform
entry_mechanism: Saudi AI company developing medical imaging AI under SDAIA and MOH data access framework
executive_summary:
Radiology AI — software that analyses medical images (X-ray, CT, MRI) to detect pathology, prioritise worklists, and assist radiologist reporting — is the fastest-growing segment of the medical device market. Saudi Arabia's radiology workforce faces a structural shortage: there are approximately 1,200 radiologists for a population of 36 million, compared to a WHO target of 2,500. AI-assisted radiology can significantly expand radiologist throughput and improve diagnostic quality in under-served regions.

Node MD-4 targets a Saudi-developed radiology AI platform — trained on Saudi clinical data, validated against Saudi patient populations, and deployed across Saudi Arabia's hospital network. The platform addresses both the workforce shortage and the data sovereignty imperative: Saudi patient imaging data should be processed by Saudi-approved algorithms on Saudi infrastructure, not sent to foreign cloud AI services.

The global AI in medical imaging market was valued at $1.4 billion in 2024 growing at 38.2% CAGR — the highest growth rate in the entire medical device market. Saudi Arabia's combination of centralised healthcare data (MOH integrated systems), strong AI research capability (SDAIA, KAUST), and significant unmet diagnostic demand creates a compelling domestic development case.

companies.name: Aidoc
companies.rationale: Aidoc is a leading radiology AI company. Subject to normalisation, but their platform architecture is the global benchmark.

companies.name: Lunit
companies.rationale: Lunit develops radiology AI for chest X-ray and mammography. Active Middle East market engagement; no ITAR concerns.

companies.name: Intelerad / Ambra Health
companies.rationale: Intelerad provides radiology workflow and PACS systems integrated with AI. Saudi deployment with Saudi AI module integration.

companies.name: Siemens Healthineers (AI-Rad Companion)
companies.rationale: Siemens has AI-powered imaging analysis tools and major Saudi hospital relationships. Platform licensing with Saudi AI customisation.
companies.contact: Siemens Healthineers VP Digital Health, Middle East

companies.name: Google Health / DeepMind Health
companies.rationale: Google Health has radiology AI research and platform capabilities. Saudi data residency requirements would need to be addressed.

partners.name: SDAIA (Saudi Data and AI Authority)
partners.current: National AI strategy; healthcare AI programme; data governance framework
partners.upgrade: SDAIA-MOH Saudi radiology AI programme; anonymised imaging data access for Saudi AI training; national radiology AI validation framework; SFDA AI medical device certification pathway
partners.timeline: 24 months to first validated Saudi radiology AI

partners.name: KAUST AI Research
partners.current: Computer vision; deep learning; medical imaging research
partners.upgrade: KAUST-MOH radiology AI research programme; Saudi-population-specific diagnostic model training; Arabic radiology reporting NLP

partners.name: King Faisal Specialist Hospital
partners.current: Advanced radiology department; PACS systems; large imaging archive
partners.upgrade: KFSH imaging data partnership for AI training; KFSH as clinical validation site; KFSH radiologists as AI performance validators

partners.name: Elm Company / Digital Health
partners.current: Government digital platforms; health IT systems
partners.upgrade: Saudi radiology AI platform development; MOH health IT integration; Saudi-operated AI inference infrastructure; SFDA SaMD (Software as Medical Device) certification
